For each assigned window, the code adds it to the merging set (potentially triggering merges), then checks if the resulting window is late via `isWindowLate(actualWindow)`\n3. In this scenario, `elementWindows` stays empty, `isSkippedElement` remains true, and `isElementLate(element)` is true, so the element should route to the side output. {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01AUyEiNgeRo2whNfLqKW3DE","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"All tests pass currently. So there might not be a test for the specific bug scenario. Let me re-read the task description again to understand the exact scenario that's broken.\n\nThe task says:\n\"Fix a bug in Apache Flink's windowing operator where late-arriving data elements that should be emitted to a side output (via `OutputTag`) are silently dropped when using session windows (merging windows). The bug occurs because the merging window operator's `processElement()` method checks lateness BEFORE merging, so an element that arrives after the watermark but could extend a session window is incorrectly classified as late and then the side output emission is skipped because the output tag is null at that point in the code path.\"\n\nAnd then later: \"The late element handling code path attempts `sideOutput()` but the output collector may not have the tag registered, causing the element to be silently dropped\"\n\nLet me think about this scenario more carefully:1. An element arrives with timestamp past the watermark (element is late)\n2. For session windows, this element could extend an existing session window (because the timestamp could be within the gap)\n3. The current code checks `isWindowLate(actualWindow)` AFTER merging\n4. If the merged window is already late, the element is dropped (line 369-371)\n5. isSkippedElement remains true\n6. Later, the code tries to side output the element (line 442)\n\nBut wait, if the window is retired (line 370), then the merge state might have an issue. Let me look at the MergingWindowSet code to understand what happens when we retire a window.\n\nActually, I think the real issue might be different. Let me think about this:\n1. An element arrives with timestamp T1\n2. It's assigned to a window W\n3. The window is merged with an existing window to get actualWindow\n4. The actualWindow is late (isWindowLate returns true)\n5. We retire the window and continue\n6. No element gets added to any window\n\n\n7. isSkippedElement stays true\n8. We try to side output at line 442\n\nIf we retire the window at line 370, the window gets removed from the merging window set, which could leave the state inconsistent when we later try to persist at line 403. The core issue seems to be that when an element's timestamp is past the watermark but could still extend an existing session window, the current logic marks it as late too early. Session windows work differently than fixed windows—an element can extend a session even if it arrives after the watermark, as long as it falls within the session gap. window becomes [min(T1, T), max(T2, T + gapSize)]. We then check if this merged window is late by comparing its end timestamp against the watermark plus allowed lateness. If max(T2, T + gapSize) + allowedLateness <= W, the window is considered late and we skip processing.\n\nI need to clarify how the SessionWindowAssigner actually works. An element with timestamp T gets assigned to window [T, T+G] where G is the gap duration. When this new window overlaps with an existing window [T1, T2], they merge into a single window spanning from the earliest to latest timestamp.\n\nFor session windows specifically, merging occurs when windows are adjacent or overlapping—that is, when T2 >= T3 for consecutive windows [T1, T2] and [T3, T4] after sorting by start time.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":10,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2462,"cache_read_input_tokens":38679,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":2462},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"70acd734-4cc0-403a-aa93-3cc5e9ec09c1","uuid":"8af54fda-8723-48b9-95e1-c916fa302c16"}

But the issue description says \"the output collector may not have the tag registered\".\n\nWait, actually I think I might have finally understood the bug! Let me re-read the processElement code one more time, very carefully.\n\nLooking at lines 303-403 (merging windows case):\n```java\nif (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n    MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n\n    for (W window : elementWindows) {\n        W actualWindow = mergingWindows.addWindow(window, mergeFunction);\n        \n        // drop if the window is already late\n        if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)) {\n            mergingWindows.retireWindow(actualWindow);\n            continue;\n        }\n        isSkippedElement = false;\n        // ... add element to window ...\n    }\n    \n    mergingWindows.persist();\n}\n```\n\nAnd then later at lines 440-446:\n```java\nif (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)) {\n    if (lateDataOutputTag != null) {\n        sideOutput(element);\n    } else {\n        this.numLateRecordsDropped.inc();\n    }\n}\n```\n\nI think I found the issue! The problem is in the order of operations:\n1. For merging windows, we iterate through elementWindows\n2. For each window, we check if the ACTUALWINDOW (after merging) is late\n3. If it is late, we retire it and continue\n\n\n4. We only set `isSkippedElement = false` if the window is NOT late\n5. Later, we check if `isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)`\n\nThe real problem is that when a window becomes late after merging, we skip adding the element to it, but we don't properly handle the case where the element itself should be routed to the late data output. An element assigned to a window that becomes late after merging gets silently dropped instead of being sent to the side output when a late data tag is configured.\n\nLet me trace through a concrete scenario: an element arrives at timestamp 1000 and gets assigned to window [1000, 1003]. At that moment, the window isn't late relative to the current watermark, so we add the element and set `isSkippedElement = false`. Then the watermark jumps to 5000, making window [1000, 1003] late. When the next element at timestamp 1500 arrives and tries to merge with existing windows, the old window gets cleaned up. The new window [1500, 1503] can't merge with anything, so it becomes a fresh window. But the original element from timestamp 1000 is already lost—it was added to a window that later became late and got discarded.\n\nWhen an element at 1200 arrives next, it gets assigned to [1200, 1203], which is immediately late relative to watermark 6000. The window gets retired without adding the element, so `isSkippedElement` stays true and we side-output it. The issue is that the first element (timestamp 1000) should have been side-output too, but it wasn't—it was silently dropped when its window was cleaned up. The bug appears to be that elements added to windows that later become late get lost without being side-output. Looking at the code path more carefully, I need to understand why the element isn't being side-output when it should be. The condition at line 441 checks if `lateDataOutputTag` exists before side-outputting, so a null tag would cause the element to be dropped entirely rather than emitted. This suggests the real problem might involve how windows are being merged and cleaned up—an element could be assigned to a window normally, but then lose its association when that window is discarded, preventing proper late data handling.\n\nLet me check if there are any existing bug reports or code comments that address this scenario.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":9,"cache_creation_input_tokens":900,"cache_read_input_tokens":47999,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":900},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"70acd734-4cc0-403a-aa93-3cc5e9ec09c1","uuid":"b6c49b55-f706-489e-89ce-9c4beffc8c98"}

Let me think about this scenario:1. Session window with gap 3, allowedLateness = 0\n2. Element at 1000, watermark at 1999 -> window [1000, 1003] is not late, element is added\n3. Element at 2000, watermark at 1999 -> window [2000, 2003] is not late, element is added\n4. These two windows merge to [1000, 2003]\n5. Watermark at 2003\n6. Element at 1500, watermark at 2003:\n   - Element is assigned to window [1500, 1503]\n   - Window [1500, 1503] merges with [1000, 2003] to get [1000, 2003]\n   - But wait, these don't overlap, so no merge happens\n   - isWindowLate([1500, 1503]) -> 1503 + 0 <= 2003? YES\n   - We retire [1500, 1503] and continue\n   - No more windows, so isSkippedElement = true\n   - isElementLate(element at 1500) -> 1500 + 0 <= 2003? YES\n   - We side output the element to late data tag\n\nOK so in this case, the element is correctly side output. So what's the bug?Let me think about another scenario:1. Session window with gap 3, allowedLateness = 0\n2. Element at 1000, watermark at 1999 -> window [1000, 1003] is added, element is added\n\n\n3. Watermark advances to 2004 -> window [1000, 1003] becomes late (1003 + 0 <= 2004) and gets cleaned up\n4. Element at 1500, watermark at 2004:\n   - Element is assigned to window [1500, 1503]\n   - No existing windows to merge with since [1000, 1003] was already removed\n   - So [1500, 1503] is treated as a new window\n   - isWindowLate([1500, 1503]) checks if 1503 + 0 <= 2004, which is true, so the window is immediately retired\n   - Since no window accepted the element, isSkippedElement = true and the element gets side output as late data\n\nNow consider a different scenario: session window with gap 3, allowedLateness = 0. Element at 1000 arrives with watermark 1999, creating window [1000, 1003]. Then element at 2000 arrives with watermark 2999, creating window [2000, 2003]. When watermark reaches 3004, both windows become late and are cleaned up. Later, element at 1500 arrives with watermark 3004. It gets assigned to window [1500, 1503], but since all previous windows were already cleaned up, there's nothing to merge with. The new window [1500, 1503] is checked: isWindowLate([1500, 1503]) evaluates whether 1503 + 0 <= 3004, which is true, so the window is immediately retired.\n\nThe element itself passes the late check since 1500 + 0 <= 3004 is also true, so it gets side output. But I'm still not seeing where the bug manifests. Let me reconsider—what if an element could actually extend an existing window instead of creating a new one? For instance, with gap 3 and allowedLateness 0: element at 1000 with watermark 1999 creates [1000, 1003], then element at 2000 with watermark 2999 creates [2000, 2003]. At watermark 3000, both windows should already be late since 1003 + 0 <= 3000 is true.\n\nActually, I need to think about this differently. With gap 10 and allowedLateness 0: element at 1000 creates [1000, 1010], then watermark advances to 1011 making that window late. But then element at 1005 arrives—it could merge with the existing window, extending it to [1000, 1015]. Now the merged window isn't late anymore since 1015 + 0 > 1011. That's the issue: the element should be added because merging extends the window past the late threshold.\n\nThe code appears to handle this correctly by checking if the merged window is late, but there might be a problem with how the merging logic actually extends the existing window [1000, 1010] when the new element arrives.\n\nLooking more carefully at the task description, the issue seems to be that an element with a timestamp past the watermark could extend a session window, but it's being incorrectly classified as late. The side output emission is then skipped because the output tag is null at that point in the code path. And if the element is marked as late and assigned to a window that gets retired, the element might not be added to any window, and then it should be side output as a late element.\n\nBut wait, I still don't see where the tag would be null...\n\nActually, let me re-read the task description one more time: \"An element arrives with a timestamp past the current watermark but could extend a session window\". When we iterate through elementWindows and check if each window is late, we're retiring windows before we've finished processing all the elements assigned to them. Element at 1000: assigned to [1000, 1010], added to window, isSkippedElement = false\n2. Element at 5000: assigned to [5000, 5010], merged with [1000, 1010] to get [1000, 5010], added, isSkippedElement = false\n3. Watermark at 5011\n4. Element at 1500: \n   - Assigned to [1500, 1510]\n   - Try to merge with [1000, 5010]\n   - [1500, 1510] and [1000, 5010] overlap (1500 < 5010), so they merge to [1000, 5010]\n   - isWindowLate([1000, 5010]) -> 5010 + 0 <= 5011? YES\n   - We retire [1000, 5010] and continue\n   - isSkippedElement remains false? NO! Wait, we need to check when isSkippedElement is set to false.Looking at the code:\n   - Line 312: `W actualWindow = mergingWindows.addWindow(window, mergeFunction)`\n   - After the addWindow call, we get the actualWindow\n   - Line 369: We check `if (isWindowLate(actualWindow))`\n   - If it's late, we retire and continue\n   - Line 373: `isSkippedElement = false` - this is INSIDE the if statement at line 369, so it's only executed if the window is NOT late\n   \nWhen we retire a window, isSkippedElement never gets set to false, so it remains false from initialization. This means the element at 1500 gets assigned to [1500, 1510], merges with the existing window, triggers retirement of the merged window, but then isSkippedElement stays false. At the end, since isSkippedElement is false, the late element check never fires and the element gets dropped entirely.\n\nActually, looking at the code again—isSkippedElement starts as true at line 299. It only becomes false when we successfully add the element to a non-late window. If every window the element touches is late and gets retired, isSkippedElement never changes from true. The side output happens when both isSkippedElement and isElementLate are true, so the element should be captured. Unless there's a case where a window isn't late itself but merges with a late window, creating a situation where the element gets added but then the merged window is immediately retired.\n\nLet me trace through a scenario: an element at 1500 gets assigned to window [1500, 1510], which isn't late initially. But when we try to merge it with an existing [1000, 5010] window, the result is [1000, 5010]. Now we check if this merged window is late—it is, so we retire it. The problem is isSkippedElement was already set to false when we added the element, but now the window containing it is gone. The element never gets side output because isSkippedElement is false, even though it should have been.\n\nActually, wait—isSkippedElement is a local variable reset at the start of each processElement call, so it should be true for the element at 1500. Let me retrace: when processing the element at 1500, isSkippedElement starts as true, gets set to false when added to a window, but then that window gets retired as late. The element is in a retired window but isSkippedElement is false, so it won't be side output.\n\nActually, I think I see it now. The element gets added to the window, the window is marked late and retired, but isSkippedElement was already set to false when the element was added. So even though the window is late, the side output check fails because isSkippedElement is false. The element ends up in a window that's being cleaned up, and it's lost because it was never side output. (the result after merging). Then I check if that window is late—if so, I retire it and move on. If not, I add the element to it.\n\nThe potential problem emerges when an element gets added to a window that's undergoing a merge. The merge callback executes during the `addWindow` call, updating the merging state and potentially triggering window emissions before the element is actually added. However, the window can't fire during the merge since the element hasn't been inserted yet. I should look for existing comments or issues in the codebase that might clarify this behavior.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":6,"cache_creation_input_tokens":481,"cache_read_input_tokens":63966,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":481},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"70acd734-4cc0-403a-aa93-3cc5e9ec09c1","uuid":"406b1ccb-4eed-4380-b679-2f1af7f7023e"}

81→/**\n    82→ * An operator that implements the logic for windowing based on a {@link WindowAssigner} and {@link\n    83→ * Trigger}.\n    84→ *\n    85→ * <p>When an element arrives it gets assigned a key using a {@link KeySelector} and it gets\n    86→ * assigned to zero or more windows using a {@link WindowAssigner}. Based on this, the element is\n    87→ * put into panes. A pane is the bucket of elements that have the same key and same {@code Window}.\n    88→ * An element can be in multiple panes if it was assigned to multiple windows by the {@code\n    89→ * WindowAssigner}.\n    90→ *\n    91→ * <p>Each pane gets its own instance of the provided {@code Trigger}. This trigger determines when\n    92→ * the contents of the pane should be processed to emit results. When a trigger fires, the given\n    93→ * {@link InternalWindowFunction} is invoked to produce the results that are emitted for the pane to\n    94→ * which the {@code Trigger} belongs.\n    95→ *\n    96→ * @param <K> The type of key returned by the {@code KeySelector}.\n    97→ * @param <IN> The type of the incoming elements.\n    98→ * @param <OUT> The type of elements emitted by the {@code InternalWindowFunction}.\n    99→ * @param <W> The type of {@code Window} that the {@code WindowAssigner} assigns.\n   100→ */\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01XSNwSyB4AQYAaGsZtrsJ6b","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→/*\n     2→ * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one\n     3→ * or more contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file\n     4→ * distributed with this work for additional information\n     5→ * regarding copyright ownership.  The ASF licenses this file\n     6→ * to you under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the\n     7→ * \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance\n     8→ * with the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     9→ *\n    10→ *     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n    11→ *\n    12→ * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    13→ * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    14→ * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    15→ * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    16→ * limitations under the License.\n    17→ */\n    18→\n    19→package org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.operators.windowing;\n    20→\n    21→import org.apache.flink.api.common.state.ListState;\n    22→import org.apache.flink.api.java.tuple.Tuple2;\n    23→import org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.assigners.MergingWindowAssigner;\n    24→import org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.windows.Window;\n    25→\n    26→import org.slf4j.Logger;\n    27→import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;\n    28→\n    29→import java.util.ArrayList;\n    30→import java.util.Collection;\n    31→import java.util.HashMap;\n    32→import java.util.List;\n    33→import java.util.Map;\n    34→import java.util.stream.Collectors;\n    35→\n    36→/**\n    37→ * Utility for keeping track of merging {@link Window Windows} when using a {@link\n    38→ * MergingWindowAssigner} in a {@link WindowOperator}.\n    39→ *\n    40→ * <p>When merging windows, we keep one of the original windows as the state window, i.e. the window\n    41→ * that is used as namespace to store the window elements. Elements from the state windows of merged\n    42→ * windows must be merged into this one state window. We keep a mapping from in-flight window to\n    43→ * state window that can be queried using {@link #getStateWindow(Window)}.\n    44→ *\n    45→ * <p>A new window can be added to the set of in-flight windows using {@link #addWindow(Window,\n    46→ * MergeFunction)}. This might merge other windows and the caller must react accordingly in the\n    47→ * {@link MergeFunction#merge(Object, Collection, Object, Collection)} and adjust the outside view\n    48→ * of windows and state.\n    49→ *\n    50→ * <p>Windows can be removed from the set of windows using {@link #retireWindow(Window)}.\n    51→ *\n    52→ * @param <W> The type of {@code Window} that this set is keeping track of.\n    53→ */\n    54→public class MergingWindowSet<W extends Window> {\n    55→\n    56→    private static final Logger LOG = LoggerFactory.getLogger(MergingWindowSet.class);\n    57→\n    58→    /**\n    59→     * Mapping from window to the window that keeps the window state. When we are incrementally\n    60→     * merging windows starting from some window we keep that starting window as the state window to\n    61→     * prevent costly state juggling.\n    62→     */\n    63→    private final Map<W, W> mapping;\n    64→\n    65→    /**\n    66→     * Mapping when we created the {@code MergingWindowSet}. We use this to decide whether we need\n    67→     * to persist any changes to state.\n    68→     */\n    69→    private final Map<W, W> initialMapping;\n    70→\n    71→    private final ListState<Tuple2<W, W>> state;\n    72→\n    73→    /** Our window assigner. */\n    74→    private final MergingWindowAssigner<?, W> windowAssigner;\n    75→\n    76→    /** Restores a {@link MergingWindowSet} from the given state. */\n    77→    public MergingWindowSet(\n    78→            MergingWindowAssigner<?, W> windowAssigner, ListState<Tuple2<W, W>> state)\n    79→            throws Exception {\n    80→        this.windowAssigner = windowAssigner;\n    81→        mapping = new HashMap<>();\n    82→\n    83→        Iterable<Tuple2<W, W>> windowState = state.get();\n    84→        if (windowState != null) {\n    85→            for (Tuple2<W, W> window : windowState) {\n    86→                mapping.put(window.f0, window.f1);\n    87→            }\n    88→        }\n    89→\n    90→        this.state = state;\n    91→\n    92→        initialMapping = new HashMap<>();\n    93→        initialMapping.putAll(mapping);\n    94→    }\n    95→\n    96→    /**\n    97→     * Persist the updated mapping to the given state if the mapping changed since initialization.\n    98→     */\n    99→    public void persist() throws Exception {\n   100→        if (!mapping.equals(initialMapping)) {\n   101→            state.update(\n   102→                    mapping.entrySet().stream()\n   103→                            .map((w) -> new Tuple2<>(w.getKey(), w.getValue()))\n   104→                            .collect(Collectors.toList()));\n   105→        }\n   106→    }\n   107→\n   108→    /**\n   109→     * Returns the state window for the given in-flight {@code Window}. The state window is the\n   110→     * {@code Window} in which we keep the actual state of a given in-flight window. Windows might\n   111→     * expand but we keep to original state window for keeping the elements of the window to avoid\n   112→     * costly state juggling.\n   113→     *\n   114→     * @param window The window for which to get the state window.\n   115→     */\n   116→    public W getStateWindow(W window) {\n   117→        return mapping.get(window);\n   118→    }\n   119→\n   120→    /**\n   121→     * Removes the given window from the set of in-flight windows.\n   122→     *\n   123→     * @param window The {@code Window} to remove.\n   124→     */\n   125→    public void retireWindow(W window) {\n   126→        W removed = this.mapping.remove(window);\n   127→        if (removed == null) {\n   128→            throw new IllegalStateException(\n   129→                    \"Window \" + window + \" is not in in-flight window set.\");\n   130→        }\n   131→    }\n   132→\n   133→    /**\n   134→     * Adds a new {@code Window} to the set of in-flight windows. It might happen that this triggers\n   135→     * merging of previously in-flight windows. In that case, the provided {@link MergeFunction} is\n   136→     * called.\n   137→     *\n   138→     * <p>This returns the window that is the representative of the added window after adding. This\n   139→     * can either be the new window itself, if no merge occurred, or the newly merged window. Adding\n   140→     * an element to a window or calling trigger functions should only happen on the returned\n   141→     * representative. This way, we never have to deal with a new window that is immediately\n   142→     * swallowed up by another window.\n   143→     *\n   144→     * <p>If the new window is merged, the {@code MergeFunction} callback arguments also don't\n   145→     * contain the new window as part of the list of merged windows.\n   146→     *\n   147→     * @param newWindow The new {@code Window} to add.\n   148→     * @param mergeFunction The callback to be invoked in case a merge occurs.\n   149→     * @return The {@code Window} that new new {@code Window} ended up in. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01QpKJP9XSeSSwPgNqAkL7Zm","type":"tool_result","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Perfect! Now I have enough information to provide a comprehensive analysis. Let me compile my findings:\n\n## Analysis of WindowOperator.processElement() Late Data Handling for Merging Windows\n\nBased on my thorough analysis of the code, here's a detailed examination of the merging window late data handling logic:\n\n### 1. **elementWindows Assignment and Merging Windows Flow (Lines 303-403)**\n\n**How elementWindows assignment works:**\n- Line 294-296: `elementWindows` is assigned by calling `windowAssigner.assignWindows()` with the element's value and timestamp\n- For merging window assigners (e.g., SessionWindows), this returns a collection of windows the element should belong to\n- Each window in `elementWindows` is then processed through the `MergingWindowSet`\n\n**When isSkippedElement is set to false:**\n- **Line 373**: `isSkippedElement = false;` is set AFTER a window passes the lateness check (`isWindowLate()` at line 369)\n- This flag remains initialized as `true` (line 299) and is only set to `false` when at least one assigned window is successfully processed\n- **Critical timing**: The flag is set INSIDE the loop AFTER confirming the window is not late, meaning if ALL windows are late, the element remains \"skipped\"\n\n**What happens when a window is retired at line 370:**\n- When `isWindowLate(actualWindow)` returns `true` (line 369), `mergingWindows.retireWindow(actualWindow)` is called\n- This removes the window from the `MergingWindowSet` mapping (see MergingWindowSet.java lines 125-131)\n- The loop then continues to the next window via `continue` (line 371)\n- **Important**: The window is removed from tracking, and `isSkippedElement` remains `true` for this iteration\n- No merge cleanup or trigger callbacks occur for late windows\n\n**How late data side output interacts with merging logic (Lines 440-446):**\n```java\nif (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)) {\n    if (lateDataOutputTag != null) {\n        sideOutput(element);\n    } else {\n        this.numLateRecordsDropped.inc();\n    }\n}\n```\n- The side output happens AFTER all merging window processing completes\n- Only triggered if `isSkippedElement` is still `true` AND the element itself is late\n- For merging windows: an element can only reach this side output if it was assigned to windows that were all already retired (late)\n\n---\n\n### 2. **Identified Potential Bugs and Issues**\n\n#### **BUG #1: Race Condition Between Window Retirement and Late Data Marking**\n\n**Scenario:**\n- An element arrives with timestamp T1, assigned to windows [W1, W2]\n- Window W1 is already late (beyond watermark + allowedLateness), gets retired at line 370\n- Window W2 is NOT late, element is added to W2, `isSkippedElement = false` at line 373\n- Element should be processed into W2\n- However, if we later check `isElementLate(element)` at line 440, we're checking element lateness, not window lateness\n\n**Impact:** This is actually correctly handled - the code correctly distinguishes between:\n- `isWindowLate()` (line 369): Check if window's end time + allowedLateness <= watermark\n- `isElementLate()` (line 440): Check if element's timestamp + allowedLateness <= watermark\n\nThe side output logic is only triggered when NO windows accept the element AND the element is late.\n\n#### **BUG #2: Missing Late Data Side Output for Elements Assigned to Merging Windows**\n\n**Critical Issue Found:**\nWhen processing merging windows at lines 303-403:\n- If an element is assigned to windows that ALL become late after merging/retirement, the element never reaches the side output check\n- Line 369-371: Late windows are retired with `continue`, never setting `isSkippedElement = false`\n- Line 440-446: Only side outputs if `isSkippedElement` is true AND `isElementLate(element)` is true\n\n**Scenario where data is lost:**\n1. Element E1 arrives with timestamp 1000, assigned to window W(900-1100)\n2. Before E1 arrives, another element E2 (timestamp 500) triggers a merge creating window W(500-1100)\n3. When E1 arrives, it would be assigned to W(900-1100), but this merges with W(500-1100) → result W(500-1100)\n4. Current watermark is 1050, allowedLateness = 50, so cleanup time = 1100 + 50 = 1150\n5. If watermark jumps to 1200: isWindowLate returns true (1150 <= 1200)\n6. Window W(500-1100) is retired at line 370, element is lost\n7. If element timestamp is not late enough to trigger side output condition independently... **the element disappears without side output**\n\n**Why this is a bug:**\n- The element is rejected because its window is late\n- But it should still be side-output as late data if `lateDataOutputTag` is configured\n- Current code only side outputs if the ELEMENT itself (not its window) is late\n\n#### **BUG #3: Inconsistency Between Merging and Non-Merging Branches**\n\nComparing lines 303-403 (merging) with lines 404-432 (non-merging):\n\n**Merging branch:**\n- Late windows: `mergingWindows.retireWindow(actualWindow)` + `continue`\n- No side output, no metrics increment\n\n**Non-merging branch:**\n- Late windows: simple `continue`\n- Also no immediate side output\n\nBoth rely on the unified side output logic at lines 440-446, which requires:\n1. `isSkippedElement == true` (element not added to ANY window)\n2. `isElementLate(element) == true` (element timestamp is late)\n\n**The problem:** An element can be \"late\" in the sense that its ASSIGNED WINDOW is late, but the ELEMENT TIMESTAMP itself might not be considered late by `isElementLate()`. In this case:\n- For non-merging windows: element is dropped silently, no side output even if tag is configured\n- For merging windows: same issue, compounded by window retirement\n\n#### **BUG #4: Window Retirement Doesn't Account for allowedLateness in Merging Scenario**\n\nIn the merge function (lines 316-365), when windows merge:\n- Lines 323-334: Check if merged window would become earlier than watermark (event-time validation)\n- But there's no explicit check that considers `allowedLateness`\n- The `isWindowLate()` check at line 369 uses `cleanupTime(window)` which includes `allowedLateness`\n\n**Potential race condition:**\n1. Windows W1 and W2 merge while processing an element\n2. Merge check passes (lines 323-334)\n3. Merged window MW is returned\n4. Immediately checked with `isWindowLate(MW)` - could be true\n5. Window state operations already happened in the merge function's callbacks (line 353: `triggerContext.onMerge()`)\n6. Then window is retired, but merge side effects already occurred\n\n---\n\n### 3. **Code Flow Summary**\n\n**For a late element assigned to merging windows:**\n\n```\n1. assignWindows() → [W1, W2, ...] (all become late after merge)\n2. For each window:\n   a. addWindow() → triggers merge if applicable\n   b. isWindowLate() → TRUE\n   c. retireWindow() → removes from mapping\n   d. continue (don't process element)\n3. isSkippedElement remains TRUE\n4. Check: isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)?\n   a. If TRUE → side output (if tag configured)\n   b. If FALSE → silently dropped\n```\n\n**Key concern:** The condition at line 440 is TOO STRICT. It requires both:\n- No window accepted the element (isSkippedElement = true)\n- The element's timestamp is individually late (isElementLate)\n\nThis misses the case where the element is rejected because ALL its windows are late.\n\n---\n\n### 4. **Related Code Issues**\n\n**No FIXMEs or TODOs found** directly related to merging windows and late data handling in WindowOperator.java.\n\nHowever, there is evidence of test coverage attempting to address this (WindowOperatorTest.java line 2040+), with explicit late data output testing, but the test suite doesn't appear to cover the merging window + late data scenario comprehensively (most test cases pass `null` for lateDataOutputTag).\n\n---\n\n### **Summary and Recommendations**\n\n**The core bug:** Elements assigned to windows that become late due to merging are not reliably side-output as late data, even when `lateDataOutputTag` is configured. The condition at line 440 requires the element itself to be individually late, not just its assigned window.\n\n**Affected use cases:**\n- Session windows with late-arriving elements\n- Interval merge windows with late data side output enabled\n- Scenarios where window merging changes the effective window lateness deadline\n\n**Recommended fix approach:**\nThe late data side output logic should account for elements that are skipped specifically because their assigned windows became late, independent of whether the element timestamp itself exceeds the lateness threshold.\n\n---\n\n### **Files Referenced:**\n- `/workspace/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperator.java` (lines 293-447)\n- `/workspace/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/MergingWindowSet.java`\n- `/workspace/flink-streaming-java/src/test/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperatorTest.java`"},{"type":"text","text":"agentId: a6155200e70b0eae7 (for resuming to continue this agent's work if needed)\n<usage>total_tokens: 27791\ntool_uses: 13\nduration_ms: 37369</usage>"}]}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"70acd734-4cc0-403a-aa93-3cc5e9ec09c1","uuid":"19c7d695-1c95-4f09-93eb-684e4bbeab21","tool_use_result":{"status":"completed","prompt":"Analyze the WindowOperator.processElement() method (lines 293-447) in /workspace/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperator.java to identify potential bugs related to late data handling for merging windows.\n\nSpecifically:\n1. `isWindowLate()` (line 369): Check if window's end time + allowedLateness <= watermark\n- `isElementLate()` (line 440): Check if element's timestamp + allowedLateness <= watermark\n\nThe side output    \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(gapSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new ReducedSessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                lateness,\n                                lateOutputTag);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> sideExpected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        // First window: [1000, 1010]\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1012));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1012));\n\n        // Merging element: creates [1000, 1015]\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 1005));\n\n        // Advance watermark past the cleanup time of [1000, 1015] (cleanup = 1015 + 5 = 1020)\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1020));\n\n        // This element should be side-output as late\n        // Element timestamp 1010 + lateness 5 = 1015, watermark is 1020\n        // So isElementLate returns true (1015 <= 1020)\n        // And the window [1010, 1020] is late (cleanup 1025 > 1020, so actually NOT late yet)\n\n        // Let me use a different approach: element that arrives when watermark\n        // has already passed the cleanup deadline\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1025));\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key1-3\", 1000L, 1015L), 1014));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1025));\n\n        // Now this element arrives late - its window [1010, 1020] is late\n        // (cleanup = 1020 + 5 = 1025 <= 1025, so it IS late)\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 1010));\n\n        // This element should be side-output as late\n        sideExpected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 1010));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>> sideActual =\n                testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"SideOutput was not correct.\",\n                sideExpected,\n                (Iterable) sideActual,\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void                           stateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new SessionWindowFunction()),\n StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 2500));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 6000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 6500));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 7000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n\n        expectedOutput.clear();\n        testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n        testHarness.setup();\n        testHarness.initializeState(snapshot);\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 4000));\n testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * This tests a custom Session window assigner that assigns some elements to \"point windows\",\n     * windows that have the same timestamp for start and end.\n     *\n     * <p>In this test, elements that have 33 as the second tuple field will be put into a point\n     * window.\n     */\n    @Test\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    void testPointSessions() throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                new PointSessionWindows(3000),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new SessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n        OperatorSubtaskState snapshot;\n\n        try (OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator)) {\n            testHarness.open();\n\n            // add elements out-of-order\n            testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 0));\n            testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 33), 1000));\n\n            // do a snapshot, close and restore again\n            snapshot = testHarness.snapshot(0L, 0L);\n        }\n\n        try (OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator)) {\n            testHarness.setup();\n            testHarness.initializeState(snapshot);\n            testHarness.open();\n\n            testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 33), 2500));\n\n            testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 10));\n            testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 1000));\n            testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 33), 2500));\n\n            testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(12000));\n\n            expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key1-36\", 10L, 4000L), 3999));\n            expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-67\", 0L, 3000L), 2999));\n            expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(12000));\n\n            TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                    \"Output was not correct.\",\n                    expectedOutput,\n                    testHarness.getOutput(),\n                    new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static <OUT>\n            OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, OUT> createTestHarness(\n                    OneInputStreamOperatorFactory<Tuple2<String, Integer>, OUT> operator) {\n        try {\n            return new KeyedOneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<>(\n                    operator, new TupleKeySelector(), BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO);\n        } catch (Exception e) {\n            throw new RuntimeException(e);\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static <OUT>\n            OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, OUT> createAsyncTestHarness(\n                    OneInputStreamOperator<Tuple2<String, Integer>, OUT> operator) {\n        try {\n            return AsyncKeyedOneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness.create(\n                    operator, new TupleKeySelector(), BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO);\n        } catch @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testCleanupTimeOverflow(boolean enableAsyncState) throws Exception {\n        final int windowSize = 1000;\n        final long lateness = 2000;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        TumblingEventTimeWindows windowAssigner =\n                TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofMillis(windowSize));\n\n        final WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                windowAssigner,\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new PassThroughWindowFunction<\n                                                String, TimeWindow, Tuple2<String, Integer>>()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                lateness,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n                        windowAssigner,\n                        EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                        new ExecutionConfig(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                        TypeInformation.of(String.class));\n        builder.allowedLateness(Duration.ofMillis(lateness));\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                testHarness =\n                        enableAsyncState\n                                ? createAsyncTestHarness(\n                                        builder.asyncReduce(\n                                                new SumReducer(),\n                                                new PassThroughWindowFunction<>()))\n                                : createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        long timestamp = Long.MAX_VALUE - 1750;\n        Collection<TimeWindow> windows =\n                windowAssigner.assignWindows(\n                        new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1),\n                        timestamp,\n                        new WindowAssigner.WindowAssignerContext() {\n                            @Override\n                            public long getCurrentProcessingTime() {\n                                return enableAsyncState\n                                        ? ((AsyncWindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>)\n                                                        testHarness.getOperator())\n                                                .getWindowAssignerContext()\n                                                .getCurrentProcessingTime()\n                                        : ((WindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>)\n                                                        testHarness.getOperator())\n                                                .windowAssignerContext.getCurrentProcessingTime();\n                            }\n                        });\n        TimeWindow window = Iterables.getOnlyElement(windows);\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), timestamp));\n\n        // the garbage collection timer would wrap-around\n        assertThat(window.maxTimestamp() + lateness).isLessThan(window.maxTimestamp());\n\n        // and it would prematurely fire with watermark (Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500)\n        assertThat(window.maxTimestamp() + lateness).isLessThan(Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500);\n\n        // if we don't correctly prevent wrap-around in the garbage collection\n        // timers this watermark will clean our window state for the just-added\n        // element/window\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500));\n\n        // this watermark is before the end timestamp of our only window\n        assertThat(window.maxTimestamp()).isStrictlyBetween(Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500, Long.MAX_VALUE);\n\n        // push in a watermark that will trigger computation of our window\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(window.maxTimestamp()));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500));\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), window.maxTimestamp()));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(window.maxTimestamp()));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expected,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(11501));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-5\", 1000L, 11500L), 11499));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(11501));\n\n StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-1\", 11600L, 14600L), 14599));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        // this is side output as late\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 10000));\n        sideExpected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 10000));\n\n        // this is also side output as late (we test that they are not accidentally merged)\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 10100));\n        sideExpected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 10100));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 14500));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-1\", 14500L, 17500L), 17499));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(100000));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(100000));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>> sideActual =\n                testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"SideOutput was not correct.\",\n                sideExpected,\n                (Iterable) sideActual,\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void testSideOutputDueToLatenessSessionZeroLateness() throws Exception {\n     // same as testSideOutputDueToLatenessSessionWithLateness() but with an accumulating\n        // trigger, i.e.\n        // one that does not return FIRE_AND_PURGE when firing but just FIRE. The expected\n        // results are therefore slightly different.\n\n        final int gapSize = 3;\n        final long lateness = 10;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(gapSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new ReducedSessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                lateness,\n                                lateOutputTag /* late data output tag */);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1999));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 2000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        // this will not be sideoutput because the session we're adding two has maxTimestamp\n        // after the current watermark\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 4500));\n\n        // new session\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 8500));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(7400));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(7400));\n\n        // this will merge the two sessions into one\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 7000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(11501));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-5\", 1000L, 11500L), 11499));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(11501));\n\n        // new session\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 11600));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-1\", 11600L, 14600L), 14599));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        // because of the small allowed lateness and because the trigger is accumulating\n        // this will be merged into the session (11600-14600) and therefore will not\n        // be sideoutput as late\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 10000));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 14500));\n\n        // adding (\"key2\", 1) extended the session to (10000-146000) for which\n        // maxTimestamp <= currentWatermark. Therefore, we immediately get a firing\n        // with the current version of EventTimeTrigger/EventTimeTriggerAccum\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-2\", 10000L, 14600L), 14599));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>> sideActual =\n                testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        assertThat(sideActual).isNull();\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-3\", 10000L, 17500L), 17499));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(100000));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(100000));\n\n        actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        sideActual = testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        assertThat(sideActual).isNull();\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testCleanupTimerWithEmptyListStateForTumblingWindows2(boolean enableAsyncState)\n            throws Exception {\n        final int windowSize = 2;\n        final long lateness = 100;\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> windowStateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        String,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                windowStateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new PassThroughFunction2()),\n                                new EventTimeTriggerAccumGC(lateness),\n                                lateness,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n                        TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                        new EventTimeTriggerAccumGC(lateness),\n                        new ExecutionConfig(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                        TypeInformation.of(String.class));\n        builder.allowedLateness(Duration.ofMillis(lateness));\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String> testHarness =\n                enableAsyncState\n                        ? createAsyncTestHarness(builder.asyncApply(new PassThroughFunction2()))\n                        : createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        // normal element\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1599));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(2100));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(5000));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1599));\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(\"GOT: (key2,1)\", 1999));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1999)); // here it fires and purges\n        expected.add(new Watermark(2100)); // here is the cleanup timer\n        expected.add(new Watermark(5000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expected,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    private static class PassThroughFunction2\n ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    //  UDFs\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    private static class PassThroughFunction\n            implements WindowFunction<\n                    Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public void apply(\n                String k,\n                TimeWindow window,\n                Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>> input,\n                Collector<Tuple2<String, Integer>> out)\n                throws Exception {\n            for (Tuple2<String, Integer> in : input) {\n                out.collect(in);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class EmptyReturnFunction\n            implements WindowFunction<\n                    Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public void apply(\n                String k,\n                TimeWindow window,\n                Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>> input,\n                Collector<Tuple2<String, Integer>> out)\n                throws Exception {}\n    }\n\n    private static class SumReducer implements ReduceFunction<Tuple2<String, Integer>> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public Tuple2<String, Integer> reduce(\n                Tuple2<String, Integer> value1, Tuple2<String, Integer> value2) throws Exception {\n            return new Tuple2<>(value2.f0, value1.f1 + value2.f1);\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class RichSumReducer<W extends Window>\n            extends RichWindowFunction<\n                    Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, W> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        private boolean openCalled = false;\n\n        @Override\n        public void open(OpenContext openContext) throws Exception {\n            super.open(openContext);\n            openCalled = true;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void close() throws Exception {\n            super.close();\n            closeCalled.incrementAndGet();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void apply(\n                String key,\n                W window,\n                Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>> input,\n                Collector<Tuple2<String, Integer>> out)\n                throws Exception {\n\n            assertThat(openCalled).as(\"Open was not called\").isTrue();\n\n            int sum = 0;\n\n            for (Tuple2<String, Integer> t : input) {\n                sum += t.f1;\n            }\n            out.collect(new Tuple2<>(key, sum));\n        }\n    }\n\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    private static class Tuple2ResultSortComparator implements Comparator<Object>, Serializable {\n        @Override\n        public int compare(Object o1, Object o2) {\n            if (o1 instanceof Watermark || o2 instanceof Watermark) {\n                return 0;\n            } else {\n                StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>> sr0 =\n                        (StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>) o1;\n                StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>> sr1 =\n                        (StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>) o2;\n                if (sr0.getTimestamp() != sr1.getTimestamp()) {\n                    return (int) (sr0.getTimestamp() - sr1.getTimestamp());\n                }\n                int comparison = sr0.getValue().f0.compareTo(sr1.getValue().f0);\n                if (comparison != 0) {\n                    return comparison;\n                } else {\n                    return sr0.getValue().f1 - sr1.getValue().f1;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    private static class Tuple3ResultSortComparator implements Comparator<Object>, Serializable {\n        @Override\n        public int compare(Object o1, Object o2) {\n            if (o1 instanceof Watermark || o2 instanceof Watermark) {\n                return 0;\n            } else {\n                StreamRecord<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>> sr0 =\n                        (StreamRecord<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>) o1;\n                StreamRecord<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>> sr1 =\n                        (StreamRecord<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>) o2;\n                if (sr0.getTimestamp() != sr1.getTimestamp()) {\n                    return (int) (sr0.getTimestamp() - sr1.getTimestamp());\n                }\n                int comparison = sr0.getValue().f0.compareTo(sr1.getValue().f0);\n                if (comparison != 0) {\n                    return comparison;\n                } else {\n                    comparison = (int) (sr0.getValue().f1 - sr1.getValue().f1);\n                    if (comparison != 0) {\n                        return comparison;\n                    }\n                    return (int) (sr0.getValue().f2 - sr1.getValue().f2);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class TupleKeySelector implements KeySelector<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public String getKey(Tuple2<String, Integer> value) throws Exception {\n            return value.f0;\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class SessionWindowFunction\n            implements WindowFunction<\n                    Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>, String, TimeWindow> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public void apply(\n                String key,\n                TimeWindow window,\n                Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>> values,\n                Collector<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>> out)\n                throws Exception {\n            int sum = 0;\n            for (Tuple2<String, Integer> i : values) {\n                sum += i.f1;\n            }\n            String resultString = key + \"-\" + sum;\n            out.collect(new Tuple3<>(resultString, window.getStart(), window.getEnd()));\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class ReducedSessionWindowFunction\n            implements WindowFunction<\n                    Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>, String, TimeWindow> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public void apply(\n                String key,\n                TimeWindow window,\n                Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>> values,\n                Collector<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>> out)\n                throws Exception {\n            for (Tuple2<String, Integer> val : values) {\n                out.collect(new Tuple3<>(key + \"-\" + val.f1, window.getStart(), window.getEnd()));\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class SessionProcessWindowFunction\n            extends ProcessWindowFunction<\n                    Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>, String, TimeWindow> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public void process(\n                String key,\n                Context context,\n                Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>> values,\n                Collector<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>> out)\n                throws Exception {\n            int sum = 0;\n            for (Tuple2<String, Integer> i : values) {\n                sum += i.f1;\n            }\n            String resultString = key + \"-\" + sum;\n            TimeWindow window = context.window();\n            out.collect(new Tuple3<>(resultString, window.getStart(), window.getEnd()));\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class ReducedProcessSessionWindowFunction\n            extends ProcessWindowFunction<\n                    Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>, String, TimeWindow> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public void process(\n                String key,\n                Context context,\n                Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>> values,\n                Collector<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>> out)\n                throws Exception {\n            TimeWindow window = context.window();\n            for (Tuple2<String, Integer> val : values) {\n                out.collect(new Tuple3<>(key + \"-\" + val.f1, window.getStart(), window.getEnd()));\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class PointSessionWindows extends EventTimeSessionWindows {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        private PointSessionWindows(long sessionTimeout) {\n            super(sessionTimeout);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n        public Collection<TimeWindow> assignWindows(\n                Object element, long timestamp, WindowAssignerContext ctx) {\n            if (element instanceof Tuple2) {\n                Tuple2<String, Integer> t2 = (Tuple2<String, Integer>) element;\n                if (t2.f1 == 33) {\n                    return Collections.singletonList(new TimeWindow(timestamp, timestamp));\n                }\n            }\n            return Collections.singletonList(new TimeWindow(timestamp, timestamp + sessionTimeout));\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * A trigger that fires at the end of the window but does not purge the state of the fired\n     * window. This is to test the state garbage collection mechanism.\n     */\n    private static class EventTimeTriggerAccumGC extends Trigger<Object, TimeWindow> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        private long cleanupTime;\n\n        public EventTimeTriggerAccumGC(long cleanupTime) {\n            this.cleanupTime = cleanupTime;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public TriggerResult onElement(\n                Object element, long timestamp, TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx)\n                throws Exception {\n            if (window.maxTimestamp() <= ctx.getCurrentWatermark()) {\n                // if the watermark is already past the window fire immediately\n                return TriggerResult.FIRE;\n            } else {\n                ctx.registerEventTimeTimer(window.maxTimestamp());\n                return TriggerResult.CONTINUE;\n            }\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public TriggerResult onEventTime(long time, TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx) {\n            return time == window.maxTimestamp() || time == window.maxTimestamp() + cleanupTime\n                    ? TriggerResult.FIRE_AND_PURGE\n                    : TriggerResult.CONTINUE;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public TriggerResult onProcessingTime(long time, TimeWindow window, TriggerContext So this wouldn't expose the bug.\n\nLet me think differently. The bug is when isElementLate returns false even though the window is late. For that to happen, I need:\n- Window is late (e.g., cleanup time = window.maxTimestamp + allowedLateness <= watermark)\n- Element is NOT late (element.timestamp + allowedLateness > watermark)\n\nFor example:\n- gap=100, allowedLateness=10\n- Element E1 at 1000 -> window [1000, 1100]\n- Watermark at 1110 -> cleanup time = 1100 + 10 = 1110, so window IS late\n\n\n- Element E2 at 1105 -> window [1105, 1205]\n  - isWindowLate([1105, 1205]) -> 1205 + 10 = 1215 <= 1110? NO, so window is NOT late yet!\n\nSo that scenario doesn't work either. I need to reconsider what the agent was pointing out. The core issue is that an element can have an assigned window that's considered late, but the element's timestamp itself might not satisfy the isElementLate condition.\n\nFor this to occur with merging, consider: Element E1 at 1000 maps to window [1000, 1100], while element E2 at 50 maps to window [50, 150]. These don't merge since 150 < 1000. When the watermark reaches 160, window [50, 150] becomes late (150 + 0 <= 160) and gets retired. But if element E3 arrives at 155 with window [155, 255], that window isn't late yet since 255 + allowedLateness exceeds the watermark.\n\nI should focus on a simpler test case instead—just verify that a late-arriving element gets properly routed to the side output.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2222,"cache_read_input_tokens":78817,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":2222},"output_tokens":6,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"70acd734-4cc0-403a-aa93-3cc5e9ec09c1","uuid":"6dc0b876-20cd-45d6-9a90-4042a3d141b6"}

STRING_INT_TUPLE =\n            TypeInformation.of(new TypeHint<Tuple2<String, Integer>>() {});\n\n    // For counting if close() is called the correct number of times on the SumReducer\n    private static AtomicInteger closeCalled = new AtomicInteger(0);\n\n    // late arriving event OutputTag<StreamRecord<IN>>\n    private static final OutputTag<Tuple2<String, Integer>> lateOutputTag =\n            new OutputTag<Tuple2<String, Integer>>(\"late-output\") {};\n\n    private void testSlidingEventTimeWindows(\n            HarnessProvider<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>> harnessProvider)\n            throws Exception {\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                testHarness = harnessProvider.create();\n\n        testHarness.setup();\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        // add elements out-of-order\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 3999));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 3000));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 20));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 0));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 999));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1998));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1999));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 1999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 1999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(1999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(2999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 2999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 2999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(2999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        // do a snapshot, close and restore again\n        OperatorSubtaskState snapshot = testHarness.snapshot(0L, 0L);\n        testHarness.close();\n\n        expectedOutput.clear();\n        testHarness = harnessProvider.create();\n        testHarness.setup();\n        testHarness.initializeState(snapshot);\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(3999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 5), 3999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(3999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(4999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 4999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(4999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(5999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 5999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(5999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        // those don't have any effect...\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(6999));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(7999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(6999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(7999));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testSlidingEventTimeWindowsReduce(boolean enableAsyncState) throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        final int windowSize = 3;\n        final int windowSlide =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                SlidingEventTimeWindows.of(\n                                        Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize),\n
